paragraphs: Weekly Meal Plan Generation Algorithm Specification
DOC-13 | Version 1.0 | Meal Planning RE Technical Docs v1
1. Purpose and Scope

Defines the deterministic algorithm that generates a 7-day class-first meal plan from household profile, cohort/persona assignment, state/city overlay, diet constraints, cook capability, and weekday/weekend rules.

This document is part of the canonical 28-document technical system. It should be read in the order defined in DOC-00. It is written for an AI coding agent that must implement the agreed recommendation engine exactly, without inventing a competing architecture.
2. Applicable Build Series
3. Core Implementation Invariants
Always build a household profile before generating any plan.
Always map user-facing cohort answers into backend personas and overlays; do not expose all backend personas in onboarding.
Always generate a 7-day class-first plan before selecting dishes.
Always keep primary family meal classes separate from member-specific add-on components.
Never allow kid, elderly, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetic, or recovery add-on classes to replace the main family meal unless the household itself has only that member segment.
Always apply hard constraints before scoring: diet type, allergies, Jain/fasting rules, and Never list.
Always use home state and current city as separate signals; do not collapse them into one location field.
Always use class-to-dish mapping keyed by meal_class_code; never mix dishes from unrelated meal classes.
Use v3 workbook IDs as canonical seed IDs unless a later approved source-data version supersedes them.
4. Algorithm Overview
Build or load household profile.
Apply hard constraints.
Assign persona and overlays.
Find cohort rows in Cohort_Matrix_v3.
Load weekly class plan rows from Weekly_Class_Plan_v3.
Apply state profile and current city migration overlay.
Apply cook capability and time pressure adjustments.
Apply weekday/weekend cadence.
Validate class rotation and variety.
Generate add-on component plan for member segments.
Expand classes to dish candidates.
Return class-first plan plus ranked dishes and explanation tags.
5. Decision Hierarchy
6. Pseudocode

def generate_weekly_class_plan(profile):    constraints = resolve_constraints(profile)    persona = assign_persona(profile)    cohort = find_best_cohort(profile, persona)    base_plan = load_weekly_plan(cohort.cohort_id)    plan = apply_state_profile(base_plan, profile.home_state)    plan = apply_city_overlay(plan, profile.current_city, profile.home_state)    plan = apply_cook_capability(plan, profile.cook_capability)    plan = apply_weekday_weekend_rules(plan)    plan = validate_variety(plan, profile.repeat_tolerance)    addons = generate_member_addons(plan, profile.household_members)    candidates = expand_classes_to_dishes(plan, constraints)    ranked = score_candidates(candidates, profile, plan, addons)    return assemble_week_plan(plan, addons, ranked)
7. Failure Handling
8. Output Shape

week_plan_day = {  "date": "2026-06-15",  "day_of_week": "Monday",  "breakfast": {"primary_class_code": "BF_LIGHT_GRAIN", "dish_candidates": [...]},  "lunch": {"primary_class_code": "LUNCH_DAL_SABZI_ROTI", "dish_candidates": [...]},  "snack": {"primary_class_code": "SNACK_LIGHT_HOME", "dish_candidates": [...]},  "dinner": {"primary_class_code": "DIN_SIMPLE_SABZI_DAL", "dish_candidates": [...]},  "addons": [...]}
9. AI Coding Agent Instructions
Read DOC-00 first to understand build sequence and document dependencies.
Use this document when implementing: BUILD-04, BUILD-05, BUILD-06.
Treat all IDs in source workbook v3 as canonical seed data identifiers.
When logic and data conflict, follow the rule hierarchy: hard constraints > add-on separation > weekly class matrix > state/city overlay > dish scoring > randomization.
Do not create new meal class codes, persona codes, or enum values in application code. Additions must go through DOC-27 Admin CMS and DOC-28 governance.
Write unit tests based on the acceptance criteria in this document and DOC-25.
10. Acceptance Criteria
Algorithm is deterministic with fixed seed.
Plan can be generated even with minimal profile.
All output classes exist in DOC-05/DOC-12.
All add-ons are separate from primary meals.
11. Change Log
tables: Defines the deterministic algorithm that generates a 7-day class-first meal plan from household profile, cohort/persona assignment, state/city overlay, diet constraints, cook capability, and weekday/weekend rules.
DOC-03, DOC-06, DOC-09, DOC-12, DOC-14, DOC-18
Claude / AI coding agent, product owner, backend developer, data operations, QA
Generate meal classes first; expand to dishes second; attach member add-ons separately.
Generate 7-day meal-class plan from cohort matrix, regional rules, city overlay, weekday/weekend rhythm, variety rules.
Attach infant/toddler/child/elderly/pregnancy/postpartum/diabetic/fitness add-ons without replacing family primary meal.
Dish Expansion and Food DNA Ranking
Expand selected meal classes into dish candidate pools and rank dishes using Food DNA + constraints + context.
Prevents special member needs from hijacking family meal.
Use nearest persona within same state; if none, use state + main cohort default.
Use secondary class, then tertiary, then safe default class.
Return class only and flag data gap for admin CMS.
Swap lower-priority repeated class with allowed secondary/tertiary class.
Use home state tier2 baseline or state capital proxy.
Initial canonical technical document generated from agreed v3 workbook and documentation architecture.
